manifest.name: ripgrep - Rust Blast Radius
manifest.description: This manifest maps the blast radius of changes across a Rust workspace by analyzing dependency depth and coupling. It tracks `dependents_count`, `coupling_risk`, and `exports_to` to predict the impact of modifying specific files and identify critical nodes in the graph.

analyzers.name: rust-depmap::file-content::default
analyzers.description: Maps Rust file dependencies, blast radius, coupling risk, and semantic context across a workspace by combining structural AST analysis with brain-enriched metadata.

fields.name: file_path
fields.description: The full path to the file being analyzed.

fields.name: file_name
fields.description: The name of the file including its extension.

fields.name: file_extension
fields.description: The file extension without the leading dot (e.g., `rs`), or `null` if none.

fields.name: chat_id
fields.description: The unique identifier for the file in the current chat context.

fields.name: crate_name
fields.description: The owning Rust crate name (e.g., `grep-searcher`).

fields.name: file_kind
fields.description: The kind of Rust file — one of `lib`, `bin`, `test`, `example`, `bench`, `build-script`.

fields.name: defines_symbols
fields.description: Symbols defined in this file (structs, enums, fns, traits, types, consts).

fields.name: imports_symbols
fields.description: Symbols imported by this file from workspace crates only.

fields.name: imports_from
fields.description: File paths this file imports from (workspace files only).

fields.name: exports_to
fields.description: File paths that import from this file (workspace files only, excluding test-consumer edges).

fields.name: reexports
fields.description: Symbols re-exported via `pub use`.

fields.name: feature_gates
fields.description: `#[cfg(feature = "...")]` gates found on items in this file.

fields.name: role
fields.description: Classification of this file's role in the dependency graph — one of `defines`, `defines-api`, `load-bearing-consumer`, `test-consumer`, `re-exports`, `wires`, `helper-consumer`, `consumer`.

fields.name: dependents_count
fields.description: Number of files with load-bearing-consumer edges to this file.

fields.name: dependencies_count
fields.description: Number of non-test, non-external files this file imports from.

fields.name: coupling_risk
fields.description: Derived coupling risk level — `low` (dependents < 2), `medium` (dependents >= 2), `high` (dependents >= 5 OR has_public_api AND dependents >= 2).

fields.name: is_entry_point
fields.description: True if no internal files import this file.

fields.name: is_leaf
fields.description: True if this file imports no internal workspace files.

fields.name: has_public_api
fields.description: True if this file exports any `pub` items.

fields.name: purpose
fields.description: Inherited from the `code-intent` brain — describes what the file does.

fields.name: surface_area
fields.description: Inherited from the `agent-file-triage` brain — e.g., `primary-implementation`, `helper`, `test`.

fields.name: change_risk
fields.description: Inherited from the `agent-file-triage` brain — `low`, `medium`, `high`.

fields.F19: Modify this file to change the test coverage for binary file detection, specifically regarding memory maps, NUL byte handling, and implicit/explicit search behaviors.

Validates end-to-end behavior of the search tool, including encoding handling, ignore rules, and output formatting flags. Use this file to change integration test coverage for CLI arguments and edge cases.
Modify this file to change the test haystack data used for verifying search and line-ending logic.
Modify this file to change the validation logic for ripgrep's JSON output format, including tests for replacements, non-UTF8 data, and CRLF handling.
Modify this file to change the declarative syntax for integration tests, including how test environments are set up and how assertion failures are reported.

Modify this file to change the integration test suite for miscellaneous ripgrep features not tied to specific regression issues. It validates behaviors like file type filtering, glob patterns, context lines, compression handling, and ignore rules using the `rgtest` macro.
Use this file to modify test cases for multiline search features, including overlapping matches, dot-newline semantics, and output formatting.
Modify this file to add or update regression tests for specific ripgrep behaviors, such as gitignore handling, symlink traversal, and regex matching.
Modify this file to change the structure or inclusion of test categories in the ripgrep test suite.

Modify this file to change the setup of integration test environments, including temporary directory creation, file generation, and the execution of the ripgrep binary under various configurations like PCRE2 or cross-compilation runners.
Modify this file to change the fuzzing strategy for `globset::Glob` construction and string representation consistency.
Modify this file to change the core line-by-line search loop, binary detection logic, and context accumulation strategy.

Implements specific search strategies (line-oriented and multi-line) that coordinate between the core search logic and input sources (readers or slices), handling binary detection and context management. Use this file to change how search loops are executed or how matches are grouped and sunk.
Modify this file to change the heuristics and safety constraints for using memory maps during file searching.
Modify this file to change the core search execution logic, configuration options, or strategy selection for memory mapping and binary detection.
Modify this file to change the core search execution logic, builder configuration, or exported types for the searcher crate.

Manages a fixed-capacity byte buffer for efficient line-oriented reading from arbitrary sources, implementing rolling buffers, binary detection heuristics, and dynamic memory allocation strategies. Modify this file to change how data is buffered, how binary bytes are handled, or the default memory allocation limits.
Modify this file to change low-level line iteration and manipulation routines for byte slices, including counting, locating, and finding preceding lines based on terminators.
Modify this file to change how test assertion failures for long strings are formatted and displayed.
Modify this file to change how search results are processed and reported to the consumer via the push-based Sink trait.
Modify this file to change how search strategies are tested, including the mock matcher behavior, the data captured by the sink, and the specific configurations exercised by the test harness.
Use this file to change the example implementation for searching stdin using the searcher and regex crates.
Modify this file to change the analysis logic for regular expression Abstract Syntax Trees (AST), specifically the detection of uppercase literals and literal characters for smart case matching.
Modify this file to change the logic for detecting and rejecting specific bytes within regex patterns.
Modify this file to change how regex patterns are parsed, configured, and transformed into HIR, including size limits, case sensitivity rules, and line terminator handling.
Modify this file to change error handling logic for regex parsing, compilation, and validation failures.
Modify this file to change the public API exports of the regex matcher and error types.

Modify this file to change the heuristic logic for extracting inner literals from regex patterns to optimize search performance. It identifies specific strings within a regex to build a faster, simplified search regex, utilizing limits to prevent excessive literal generation.
Modify this file to change how regular expressions are compiled, configured, and executed, including optimization strategies for finding candidate lines.
Modify this file to change how non-matching byte sets are derived from the regex HIR for search optimization.
Modify this file to change how regex syntax is sanitized for single-line matching modes.
Modify this file to change the available hyperlink aliases and their URL formats for different editors.
Modify this file to change how hyperlink formats are parsed, validated, and interpolated with environment variables for terminal output.
Modify this file to change how terminal color specifications are parsed, validated, and merged for output styling.
Modify this file to change how output bytes are counted and tracked, including the behavior of the writer wrapper.
Modify this file to change how search results are serialized to JSON lines, including handling of binary data, UTF-8 encoding, and match statistics.
Modify this file to change how search matches and metadata are structured and serialized to JSON using zero-allocation types.

Modify this file to change the public API exports and global constants for the printer crate. It re-exports the primary printer types (Standard, JSON, Summary) and configuration utilities (ColorSpecs, HyperlinkConfig).
Modify this file to change the formatting or behavior of the custom test assertion macro used for comparing long strings.
Modify this file to change how file paths are formatted, colored, and terminated in the terminal output, including separator normalization and hyperlink support.

Modify this file to change the implementation of the standard grep-like output formatter. It handles result formatting, color application, multi-line match support, and search statistics via a builder pattern.
Modify this file to change how search performance metrics are tracked, aggregated, and serialized for output.
Modify this file to change how aggregate search statistics and match counts are displayed, including support for different summary modes and output styling.

Modify this file to change utility functions for output formatting, including string replacement, path transformation, and efficient number-to-string conversion. It handles amortized allocation for replacements and platform-specific path encoding for the printer module.
Modify this file to change how regex parsing and compilation errors are reported and structured within the PCRE2 integration.

Acts as the library entry point for the PCRE2 matcher implementation, re-exporting the core matcher, error types, and underlying PCRE2 bindings for integration with the grep ecosystem. Use this file to modify the public API surface of the PCRE2 integration crate.

Implements the `Matcher` trait using PCRE2, providing builders for configuring regex compilation options like case sensitivity, JIT, and UTF modes. Modify this file to change how PCRE2 patterns are compiled and executed within the grep framework.
Modify this file to add or update test cases for the regex matcher, specifically covering iteration, capturing, and replacement behaviors.
Modify this file to change the structure or inclusion of matcher test modules and utilities.
Modify this file to change the test adapter implementation for the regex engine or the capture group handling.
Modify this file to change how capture group references (e.g., `$1`, `$name`) are parsed and resolved within replacement strings during regex substitution.
Modify this file to change the core interface contract for all search implementations in the project.
Modify this file to update or add test cases for gitignore path matching logic, ensuring rules are correctly applied to files and directories relative to the root.
Modify this file to change the test coverage for Byte-Order Mark (BOM) handling in gitignore files. It verifies that the GitignoreBuilder correctly skips the BOM to match Git's behavior.
Modify this file to update the default mapping of file extensions and glob patterns to recognized file types (e.g., Rust, Python, Markdown).
Modify this file to change how ignore rules are loaded, matched, and prioritized during directory traversal, including support for custom ignore files and git integration.
Modify this file to change how file paths are matched against ignore patterns and globs according to gitignore specifications.

Defines the core error handling types, glob match results, and public API exports for the `ignore` crate, which manages recursive directory traversal and filtering. Modify this file to change how traversal errors are classified, how glob matches are represented, or which modules are publicly exposed.
Modify this file to change how glob-based file overrides are constructed, matched, and how case sensitivity is handled.
Modify this file to change how hidden files are detected or how path prefixes and filenames are extracted across different operating systems.
Modify this file to change how file types are defined, selected, or matched against globs.
Modify this file to change the recursive directory traversal logic, including parallel execution strategies, ignore rule application, and symlink handling.
Modify this file to change the demonstration of directory traversal strategies, including parallel and sequential walking using the `ignore` crate.

Acts as a high-level facade that re-exports the core constituent crates (`cli`, `matcher`, `searcher`, etc.) comprising ripgrep's searching routines. Modify this file to alter the public API surface or the composition of the library's exposed modules.
Modify this file to change the example implementation of recursive file searching and match printing using the grep crate.
Modify this file to change the FNV hashing algorithm implementation or the HashMap type alias configuration.
Modify this file to change how glob patterns are interpreted, optimized, or converted to regex.
Modify this file to change how glob patterns are parsed, compiled into optimized regex automata, and matched against file paths using strategies like literal, extension, or suffix matching.
Modify this file to change how file names and extensions are extracted or how path separators are normalized for glob matching.
Modify this file to change how Glob and GlobSet objects are serialized to and deserialized from JSON formats.

Benchmarks the performance of glob pattern matching, comparing the legacy `glob` crate against the optimized `globset` implementation for various path lengths and pattern complexities. Use this file to measure performance regressions or improvements in glob matching logic.
Modify this file to change how the short and long help documentation is generated, formatted, and rendered from flag definitions and templates.
Modify this file to change the `roff` formatting logic or the structure of the generated man page documentation for CLI flags.
Modify this file to change how documentation is generated or how custom markup tags within doc strings are processed.

Generates version strings and reports CPU feature support (SIMD) and build configuration (PCRE2) for the ripgrep binary. Modify this file to change how version information is formatted or which hardware capabilities are detected and displayed.
Modify this file to change the structure and logic of the generated Bash completion script, including how flags and choices are presented to the user.
Use this file to change how completions are generated or formatted for the Fish shell.
Modify this file to add or remove shell completion modules or to update the included static encoding resources.
Modify this file to change how PowerShell completions are generated for the ripgrep CLI, including the script template and flag formatting logic.
Modify this file to change the content or structure of the generated Zsh completion script.
Modify this file to change how ripgrep loads arguments from the RIPGREP_CONFIG_PATH environment variable and parses rc files.

Modify this file to change the definition, behavior, or documentation of ripgrep's command-line flags. This file implements the `Flag` trait for every command-line option, defining their names, aliases, documentation strings, and the logic to update the `LowArgs` configuration structure.
Modify this file to change how CLI flags are translated into search configurations, matchers, and printers.
Modify this file to change the structure of low-level CLI arguments, add new flag modes, or adjust default values for search configuration options.
Modify this file to change the definition, parsing logic, or documentation generation for ripgrep's command line flags and arguments.
Modify this file to change how command-line arguments are parsed, how configuration files are merged with CLI flags, or the logic for suggesting similar flags on typos.
Modify this file to change the logic for determining if a directory entry should be treated as a search target.
Modify this file to change the global logging format or initialization behavior for the application.
Modify this file to change the high-level execution flow, allocator selection, or mode dispatching logic.
Modify this file to change how error messages are emitted, how stderr is locked, or how the global error state is tracked.
Modify this file to change the high-level search orchestration, including how preprocessors, decompression, and binary detection are integrated into the search workflow.
Modify this file to change the default decompression commands or the process spawning logic.
Modify this file to change how arbitrary bytes and invalid UTF-8 sequences are converted to human-readable escaped strings and vice versa.
Modify this file to change how the system hostname is detected or resolved for file paths.
Modify this file to change the parsing logic for human-readable size inputs or the supported suffixes and their byte multipliers.
Modify this file to change the behavior of CLI utilities such as stdin detection heuristics, stdout buffering strategies, or string escaping logic.
Modify this file to change how search patterns are validated for UTF-8 compliance and read from input sources like files or stdin.
Modify this file to change the streaming reader wrapper for external command processes, specifically the handling of asynchronous stderr reading and error surfacing.
Modify this file to change the stdout buffering strategy, color support, or terminal detection logic for the CLI.
Modify this file to change build-time metadata embedding or Windows executable linker settings.
